questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Electrochemical cells can be classified into two types.
questions.reason: Reason (R): One type is based on galvanic cells and the other is based on electrolytic cells.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The classification of electrochemical cells into galvanic and electrolytic cells is accurate.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The standard electrode potential of a half-cell is measured at standard conditions.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Standard conditions include a concentration of 1 M and a temperature of 25°C.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Standard electrode potential is indeed measured at standard conditions which include a concentration of 1 M and a temperature of 25°C.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Electrolysis involves the reduction of ions at the cathode.
questions.reason: Reason (R): The reduction of ions at the cathode results in the gain of electrons by the ions.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Electrolysis does indeed involve the reduction of ions at the cathode, which results in the gain of electrons by the ions.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The potential of a cell can be determined using the Nernst equation.
questions.reason: Reason (R): The Nernst equation takes into account the concentration of ions and the temperature.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The Nernst equation is indeed used to determine the potential of a cell, and it takes into account the concentration of ions and the temperature.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): A galvanic cell produces electricity through spontaneous redox reactions.
questions.reason: Reason (R): The spontaneous redox reactions result in a net movement of electrons from the anode to the cathode.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Galvanic cells do indeed produce electricity through spontaneous redox reactions, resulting in a net movement of electrons from the anode to the cathode.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Electrolysis is used to purify metals.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Electrolysis involves the oxidation of anions at the anode to obtain metals.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Electrolysis is indeed used to purify metals, but it involves the reduction of cations at the cathode, not the oxidation of anions at the anode.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The standard cell potential of a galvanic cell is always positive.
questions.reason: Reason (R): A positive cell potential indicates that the cell will produce electricity.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The standard cell potential of a galvanic cell is indeed always positive, and this indicates that the cell will produce electricity.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): In an electrolytic cell, the anode is negatively charged.
questions.reason: Reason (R): The anode is where oxidation takes place, and oxidation involves the loss of electrons, which results in a negative charge.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: In an electrolytic cell, the anode is indeed negatively charged because oxidation takes place there, but the reason is not because oxidation involves the loss of electrons resulting in a negative charge.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The cell potential of a galvanic cell is determined by the standard cell potential.
questions.reason: Reason (R): The cell potential is also affected by the concentration of ions and the temperature.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The cell potential of a galvanic cell is indeed determined by the standard cell potential, as well as the concentration of ions and the temperature.
